articles.headline: NIA takes over probe into killing of Manipur church leaders
articles.summary: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has taken over the investigation into the killing of three church leaders in Manipur’s Kangpokpi district in May. Armed miscreants ambushed two vehicles, leading to the deaths and triggering ethnic tension. The case was re-registered by NIA on June 8 following a Ministry of Home Affairs order.

articles.headline: CUET results out, UG admissions in Capital begin this week
articles.summary: CUET-UG results are out, and Delhi University plans to start undergraduate admissions this week, aiming for an August session. Other Central universities like Ambedkar University Delhi and Jamia Millia Islamia are also gearing up for admissions. No major changes in the process this year, with CUET scores determining admissions.

articles.headline: CM panel clears new AUD campus, Delhi road upgrades
articles.summary: Chief Minister Rekha Gupta approved the construction of a new 50-acre Dr. B.R. Ambedkar University Delhi (AUD) campus at Dheerpur, estimated at ₹1,668 crore. The panel also cleared projects worth ₹657.99 crore to strengthen 270.63 km of roads across Delhi, aiming for safer and more durable infrastructure.

articles.headline: Sood orders inspection of Delhi coaching hubs for safety
articles.summary: Delhi Home Minister Ashish Sood ordered a multi-agency inspection of coaching hubs in Mukherjee Nagar, Rajinder Nagar, and Katwaria Sarai to verify compliance with safety norms and building regulations. The directive follows recent fatal fires and collapses, prioritizing student safety through long-term measures.

articles.headline: School rape case: HC order on accused’s bail next week
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court will rule on June 29 whether to cancel the bail granted to the 57-year-old caretaker accused of raping a three-year-old girl in a west Delhi school. Delhi Police and the complainant are challenging the trial court’s May 7 bail order. The Aam Aadmi Party also alleged threats from the accused's son.

articles.headline: Fire destroys 36 structures housing Central Vista workers
articles.summary: A massive fire gutted 36 tin sheds housing around 1,000 migrant labourers working on the Central Vista project in Delhi. The blaze, possibly from an electrical short circuit, left workers without belongings. Police are investigating the exact cause of the 3 a.m. fire.

articles.headline: Work begins on 9.9-km Central Vista metro corridor
articles.summary: The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) began construction for the 9.913 km Central Vista metro corridor, extending the Magenta Line from Janakpuri West to R.K. Ashram Marg. This Phase V(A) expansion will integrate Central Secretariat as a triple interchange station, improving connectivity in central Delhi.

articles.headline: Okhla landfill to be cleared by December: Mayor Wahi
articles.summary: Mayor Pravesh Wahi announced that the Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) is on track to clear the Okhla landfill site by year-end. Twenty-three acres have been cleared, and remaining legacy waste will be disposed of within a week, with fresh waste also processed by December.

articles.headline: Police to quiz ride apps on driver verification after rape-murder
articles.summary: Delhi Police will question three app-based ride aggregators about their driver verification processes after Bashu Kumar Singh, arrested for the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl, was found to have worked for them despite a criminal record. Drivers claim profiles can be created with just an Aadhaar and driving license.

articles.headline: Anna Hazare warns fast if RTI rules amendments not revoked
articles.summary: Activist Anna Hazare gave the Maharashtra government until July 5 to withdraw amendments to the Maharashtra Right to Information (Amendment) Rules, 2026, threatening a hunger strike. He called the changes, including increased fees and limits on information, an affront to the RTI Act's spirit.

articles.headline: TN Minister touching girl athletes’ legs sparks outrage
articles.summary: A video showing Tamil Nadu Higher Education Minister P. Viswanathan touching girl athletes' outstretched legs at an event in Madurai has sparked outrage. Critics called for a public condemnation and questioned the lack of bystander intervention. Viswanathan stated it was an 'uncivilised' attempt to target him.

articles.headline: Deepender links IAS officer's arrest to RS poll
articles.summary: Congress MP Deepender Hooda linked the CBI arrest of IAS officer Pankaj Agarwal in the IDFC First Bank scam to alleged political interference during the Rajya Sabha election. Hooda claimed Agarwal, as Returning Officer, invalidated Congress votes 'at the behest of BJP' to ensure a BJP-backed candidate's victory.

articles.headline: Lack of jobs behind desperation, says Rahul on bonded labour
articles.summary: Rae Bareli MP Rahul Gandhi criticized the BJP government in Uttar Pradesh over the rescue of 12 bonded labourers in Muzaffarnagar. He linked the 'assault on human dignity' to disappearing jobs, stagnant incomes, and weakened protections for vulnerable workers, stating 'desperation grows'.

articles.headline: Bittu apologises for ‘casteist’ remarks before panel
articles.summary: Union Minister Ravneet Singh Bittu submitted a written apology to the Punjab State Scheduled Castes Commission for alleged casteist remarks during an exchange with police in May. He stated that as a Minister, he should not have uttered such words.

articles.headline: Speaker Narwekar apologises for ‘incorrect’ Marathi
articles.summary: Maharashtra Assembly Speaker Rahul Narwekar expressed regret for incorrectly uttering words while reading obituary references, attributing it to technical issues and an unclear printed copy. He clarified he had no intention to hurt sentiments, responding to criticism from MNS president Raj Thackeray.

articles.headline: Kerala Left parties lock horns with Cong. govt. over PM SHRI
articles.summary: The LDF and UDF in Kerala clashed over the PM SHRI scheme. Opposition LDF accused the ruling UDF of 'surrendering to the Sangh Parivar agenda' by pursuing the scheme, which the previous LDF government had put on hold. The government states a committee is studying the scheme.

articles.headline: Mizoram CM rejects alliance claim between ZPM and NDA
articles.summary: Mizoram Chief Minister Lalduhoma, leader of the Zoram People’s Movement (ZPM), rejected claims of a formal alliance with the BJP-led NDA. He stated that ZPM remains committed to its regional identity and sees no immediate need for formal affiliation, despite repeated invitations from PM Modi and Amit Shah.

articles.headline: BJP councillors retake oath after court order in Kerala
articles.summary: Twenty BJP councillors of the Thiruvananthapuram Municipal Corporation were directed by the Kerala High Court to retake their oath, after their original oaths in the name of local deities and 'Balidaanis' were deemed invalid. A Congress ward member was also directed to retake oath.

articles.headline: Calicut varsity says ‘no’ to research on astrology proposal
articles.summary: The University of Calicut has rejected a demand to introduce research facilities on astrology in its affiliated colleges and study centres. The steering committee on undergraduate programmes unanimously rejected the proposal, stating that 'astronomy is a scientific subject, while astrology is not related to science'.

articles.headline: U.P. man held over alleged radicalisation activities
articles.summary: A 20-year-old man from Uttar Pradesh, identified as Suhail, was arrested from Davangere district in Karnataka over alleged links with foreign handlers involved in online radicalisation activities. Suhail had arrived in Harihara about 15 days prior and was working as a painter.

articles.headline: Viksit and Surakshit: Cost of inaction on safety
articles.summary: The Lucknow fire, killing 15, highlights regulatory failures and unplanned urban growth. India's education economy, fueled by coaching centers, often disregards safety norms. The high human and economic costs of inaction demand a nationwide assessment of building safety, transforming 'Viksit Bharat' into 'Surakshit Bharat'.

articles.headline: Razor’s edge: Monsoon deficit threatens agriculture
articles.summary: India's southwest monsoon deficit has increased to 43%, with a moderate-to-strong El Niño expected. This poses risks to agriculture, reducing farm labor productivity and threatening crops like cardamom. The situation calls for adapting to water-centric strategies, reducing water-intensive crops, and improving resilience to warming.

articles.headline: PACOM, deeper meaning behind a dropped prefix
articles.summary: The U.S. reverting to 'US PACOM' from 'US INDOPACOM' suggests regional shifts deeper than a name change. India must study implications of U.S. outreach to China, diminishing Quad salience, and U.S.-Iran ceasefire. New Delhi should reconsider its policy, given Washington's shifting positions and growing regional ambitions.

articles.headline: Clearing the road to timely trauma care in India
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the right to trauma care is an integral part of the right to life under Article 21, issuing nine binding directions for an integrated trauma response system. Implementation is key, with States required to operationalize PM RAHAT, standardize ambulance compliance, and set up grievance redress systems.

articles.headline: Sustaining India’s low-fertility future
articles.summary: India's fertility rate is 1.9, below replacement level, but uneven across states. This demands creating opportunities for young workers in poorer states while strengthening income, care, and health systems for older populations. India is ageing before completing transitions that made it manageable elsewhere, requiring stronger public systems.

articles.headline: Furore over the Mekedatu project
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Assembly adopted a resolution against Karnataka's Mekedatu dam project on the Cauvery river, which would impound 67.16 TMC ft. Tamil Nadu seeks a tribunal to examine the project, citing trust deficit and the Cauvery being a deficit river. Karnataka argues it will regulate flood flows for TN.

articles.headline: NewsClick case quashed: A victory for journalism
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court quashed the FIR and money laundering proceedings against NewsClick and its editor, Prabir Purkayastha, calling the action 'mala fide and arbitrary.' The court found no FDI violations, as the RBI had approved the transaction, and no evidence of cheating or criminal conspiracy, affirming the importance of free journalism.

articles.headline: Mapping queer lives through books: identity and equality in India
articles.summary: A selection of fiction and non-fiction works offers a layered understanding of queer life in India, exploring law, personal memory, family, and workplaces. Books like 'Transforming Rights' and 'Tell My Mother I Like Boys' highlight the ongoing struggle for rights and acceptance, even as legal frameworks evolve.

articles.headline: China ready to work with India to enhance mutual trust
articles.summary: China's Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated that China is ready to work with India to enhance mutual trust, dispel doubts, and deepen cooperation. This follows Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi's meeting with PM Modi, where he expressed willingness to implement consensus and promote BRICS cooperation.

articles.headline: Nirav Modi must pay ₹108 crore to Bank of India: U.K. court
articles.summary: Fugitive diamond merchant Nirav Modi is liable to pay Bank of India over $11.5 million (₹108 crore), including interest, on a personal loan guarantee, the London High Court ruled. Modi, imprisoned and fighting extradition in a separate fraud case, disputed the enforceability of the guarantee.

articles.headline: CRPF officer suspended, 20 transferred after kin protested CAPF Bill
articles.summary: A CRPF DIG-rank officer has been suspended and 20 others transferred, allegedly for their families protesting the Central Armed Police Force Act, 2026. The officer faces an inquiry into 'politically provocative' social media messages. The move has sparked resentment among paramilitary personnel.

articles.headline: Anxious relatives search for missing workers after Kolkata tragedy
articles.summary: Family members of missing workers frantically searched for them at the Taratala warehouse collapse site and SSKM Hospital. With about 25 rescued, and five dead, many from Bihar, the exact number trapped remains unclear. Locals aided initial rescue, with one eyewitness reporting 'a strong tremor like an earthquake'.

articles.headline: Court refuses urgent hearing in Abhishek Banerjee’s case
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court rejected Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee’s plea for an urgent hearing in a case, despite his plans to travel abroad for eye treatment. Justice Saugata Bhattacharya stated the case would be listed for hearing in the normal process.

articles.headline: Kharge slams Pradhan for ‘terrorist jibe’ on students
articles.summary: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge criticized Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan for reportedly calling protesting students 'terrorists'. Kharge linked this to earlier government responses to farmer protests and demanded Pradhan's resignation, announcing 'Chhatron Ki Goonj' press conferences across 28 cities.

articles.headline: Donation theft in Ayodhya, land grab in Mahakal: Congress
articles.summary: The Congress accused the BJP of 'donation theft in Ayodhya's Ram Temple' and 'land grab in Mahakal' (Ujjain), calling it the 'new Modi model'. The party alleged embezzlement of donations and that CM Mohan Yadav's family acquired land benefitting from proposed projects in Ujjain, betraying people's faith.

articles.headline: AAP plans marches in Gujarat for jailed MLA Vasava
articles.summary: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) will organize support marches across Gujarat for MLA Chaitar Vasava, who was sentenced to seven years imprisonment in a 2023 case. AAP alleges the 'ED Party' conspired against Vasava and vows to resist such 'hooliganism' in a democracy.

articles.headline: BJP imposing vegetarianism in Bengal, says Trinamool
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress MP Derek O’ Brien criticized the West Bengal government’s decision to engage ISKCON for providing cooked meals in Kolkata schools, calling it an attempt by the BJP to impose vegetarianism and deprive children of nutrition by denying eggs in midday meals. ISKCON stated meals would include paneer.

articles.headline: House panel seeks Ministry report on Great Nicobar Project
articles.summary: The Public Accounts Committee, chaired by K.C. Venugopal, sought a report from the Union Home Ministry on the Great Nicobar Project. Concerns were raised about imperilling the fragile ecosystem, felling 1.5 crore trees, and neglecting the naval base INS Baaz's expansion, amid 'rising panic' among residents.

articles.headline: Mehbooba attends Pandit event to mend broken ties
articles.summary: Former Jammu & Kashmir Chief Minister Mehbooba Mufti attended a Pandit sacred thread investiture ceremony in south Kashmir, aiming to forge fresh bonds between communities. She stressed that 'no amount of hate or attempts to divide us can succeed', reaffirming shared history and destiny.

articles.headline: Fiancée, lover held in Ketan Agarwal murder case in Pune
articles.summary: Pune Rural Police arrested Ketan Agarwal’s fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her lover, Chetan Chaudhary, in connection with his murder at Lohagad Fort. Initially registered as an accident, the case was reclassified as pre-planned murder after investigation revealed reconnaissance of the location prior to the incident.

articles.headline: Heavy rain triggers landslides, flash flood in Arunachal Pradesh
articles.summary: Heavy rain triggered flash floods and landslides in Arunachal Pradesh, killing one person and leaving four missing. Six districts are cut off due to disrupted road connectivity. The Indian Air Force is assessing damage, and Assam has issued a high alert for downstream areas.

articles.headline: NMC to phase out PG diploma medical courses from 2027-28
articles.summary: The National Medical Commission (NMC) announced the phasing out of postgraduate diploma medical courses, with 2026-27 being the last admission year. Medical colleges must convert existing diploma seats into MD/MS degree seats from 2027-28 to standardize PG education and align qualifications with current standards.

articles.headline: Farmer group protests India-U.S. trade deal in Punjab
articles.summary: Farmers and farm laborers under Kisan Mazdoor Morcha protested against the proposed India-U.S. trade deal in Punjab, alleging it would adversely impact farmers, laborers, shopkeepers, and youth. They demand a democratic registration of opposition to the agreement's 'dangerous consequences'.

articles.headline: India and UAE explore ways to strengthen defence ties
articles.summary: India and the UAE reviewed progress under their Strategic Defence Partnership Framework, exploring ways to deepen collaboration in defence manufacturing, innovation, and maritime security. India is in talks to export military platforms, including the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile, to the UAE.

articles.headline: SC had agreed that right to associate not for foreign funds
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has consistently backed the Centre's right to regulate foreign donations, upholding the legality of Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Rules, 2026. The court declared no vested right to accept foreign donations, agreeing with the government's stance that regulations protect national interest.

articles.headline: Top court backs lower NEET-SS cut-off for govt. doctors
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stated that the NEET-SS qualifying percentile for in-service government doctors must be reduced, as they serve the public and study concurrently. Justices B.V. Nagarathna and Joymalya Bagchi emphasized that these doctors are crucial for public health and their skills benefit the larger public.

articles.headline: Investors may move to safer bets on structural challenges
articles.summary: Economists at Ambit Institutional Equities advise investing in FMCG, pharma, IT, and telecom stocks due to India facing structural challenges to its economic growth. Concerns include concentrated stock ownership in small/mid-cap stocks, uneven consumption demand, and limited fiscal space for consolidation.

articles.headline: Flipkart Minutes, Amazon Now scale up operations
articles.summary: Amazon Now and Flipkart Minutes are intensifying the quick commerce race in India by scaling up operations. Amazon Now expands to over 300 cities, while Flipkart Minutes has reached 1,000 micro fulfilment centres across 130 cities in two years, heightening competition with players like Zomato and Swiggy.

articles.headline: ISP to be released with 60-day lag with base year 2024-25
articles.summary: The new Index of Services Production (ISP), with 2024-25 as the base year, will be released monthly with a 60-day lag. The first ISP for April 2026 will be announced on July 14, serving as a new macroeconomic indicator to measure short-term changes in the services sector's growth.

articles.headline: Adani to outsource non-core activities or move to GCCs
articles.summary: The Adani Group will simplify operations by establishing a three-layer structure, changing contractor engagement, and prioritizing worker dignity. Chairman Gautam Adani announced plans to outsource non-core activities to Global Capacity Centres or third parties, targeting 3 GW data centre capacity and 10 GW nuclear energy by 2035.

articles.headline: AI coding costs to cross developer pay by 2028
articles.summary: Gartner forecasts that by 2028, AI coding costs will exceed average developer salaries due to rising large language model (LLM) token consumption and consumption-based licensing models. Organizations are underestimating the financial impact of increased token usage, which can rise faster than productivity gains if not governed.

articles.headline: BHIM UPI to offer financial products, including loans
articles.summary: NPCI BHIM Services (NBSL) announced plans to offer a bouquet of financial products, including credit cards, loans, investments, and insurance, on the BHIM payments app. Partnerships with multiple banks are underway, with Canara Bank already distributing credit cards. Monthly transaction volumes on BHIM rose to 244 million in May 2026.

articles.headline: Businessline’s MSME meet starts today in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Businessline is organizing its fifth annual MSME Conclave in Bengaluru and Coimbatore to honor the sector's achievements and crucial role in India’s economic development. The event, co-powered by Central Bank of India, will feature panel discussions and a fireside chat, celebrating small businesses and advocating for their growth.

articles.headline: Rubio commits to UAE security during Gulf tour
articles.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed the U.S.-Iran deal with UAE President Mohamed bin Zayed, renewing Washington’s commitment to the country’s security. Rubio insisted no country is allowed to impose tolls on the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Kim unveils plans for 10,000-tonne warships, nuclear navy
articles.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un announced plans to equip its navy with nuclear weapons and build larger warships, state media reported. Kim made the remarks at the commissioning of the Choe Hyon, one of two 5,000-tonne class warships launched last year in Nampho.

articles.headline: French govt. confirms Ebola virus in doctor from Congo
articles.summary: A positive case of Ebola virus has been identified in France in a doctor who recently returned from a humanitarian mission in Congo, the French Ministry of Health confirmed. The individual had worked in one of the virus transmission zones.

articles.headline: Pak. court sentences Balochistan activist to life imprisonment
articles.summary: A Pakistani court sentenced Mahrang Baloch, a prominent Balochistan rights activist, to life imprisonment on murder and terror charges. Baloch, 33, was arrested in 2025, drawing condemnation from civil liberties advocates.

articles.headline: Nuclear weapons only guarantee against global war: Kremlin
articles.summary: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov stated that nuclear weapons are the 'only' bulwark preventing the world from slipping into a global war, amid fears of a fresh arms race after the New START treaty expired. He said the global security system was 'eroding'.

articles.headline: Ukrainian strikes knock out power in Sevastopol in Crimea
articles.summary: Ukrainian drones knocked out power in Sevastopol, Russian-held Crimea, and targeted facilities in central and southern Russia. The attacks underscored the reach of Kyiv’s strikes on energy infrastructure. Sevastopol's governor said work was ongoing to restore supplies, despite Ukrainian attempts to sow panic.

articles.headline: China’s tech advancements an opportunity, not a threat: Li Qiang
articles.summary: Chinese Premier Li Qiang defended China’s technological advancements as a global opportunity, not a threat. He stated that state subsidies were not the main reason for the rapid rise of high-tech industries, amidst Western concerns over unfair competitive advantage from such support.

articles.headline: Colombia overcomes obdurate DR Congo to reach knockout
articles.summary: Colombia secured a 1-0 win against DR Congo, courtesy of Daniel Munoz's 76th-minute goal, guaranteeing its place in the World Cup knockout rounds. The South Americans dominated, but DR Congo's goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi made several sharp saves to keep the game goalless until late in the second half.

articles.headline: England left frustrated in goalless stalemate with Ghana
articles.summary: England suffered a FIFA World Cup setback, held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by Ghana. Despite dominating possession and having 19 shots, Thomas Tuchel's side rarely looked like scoring. The result leaves England with work still to do to progress as Group L winner, raising doubts about their tournament chances.

articles.headline: At 41, Ronaldo delivers brace, ends barren run
articles.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice as Portugal defeated Uzbekistan, ending a long barren run and silencing critics. At 41 years and 138 days, he became the second-oldest goal-scorer in World Cup history. Coach Roberto Martinez praised Ronaldo's icon status and continued contribution to the team.

articles.headline: Japan, Sweden ready to joust with qualification on the line
articles.summary: Group F remains delicately poised as Japan and Sweden face off at the Dallas Stadium with qualification on the line. Japan, with momentum after a 4-0 demolition of Tunisia, needs a draw. Sweden, despite a turbulent journey, is well-placed on three points, with both teams controlling their destinies.

articles.headline: Shafali Verma declares herself an all-rounder
articles.summary: Shafali Verma, after bowling the first over for India against South Africa, now identifies herself as an all-rounder. She enjoys bowling and aims to serve the team in any situation, revealing her father encouraged her to bowl from a young age. Verma expressed confidence for the upcoming match against Bangladesh.

articles.headline: DR Congo’s living statue fan makes World Cup debut
articles.summary: Michel Nkuka Mboladinga, DR Congo’s famous 'living statue' fan known for posing as Patrice Lumumba, made his World Cup debut at the match against Colombia. He had gained fame during the AFCON for his unique tribute, and finally attended a World Cup game after missing the opener against Portugal.

articles.headline: England sets dubious distinction for most goalless draws
articles.summary: England set an unwanted record for the most goalless draws in FIFA World Cup history after their 0-0 stalemate against Ghana. Their total number of goalless matches in FIFA World Cup history has now risen to 13, creating a dubious distinction for the team.

articles.headline: Record for most goals scored in group stage broken
articles.summary: The first 48 matches of the 2026 World Cup produced 141 goals, breaking the previous record of 136 goals set during the 2014 World Cup group stage. This new tally represents the highest in the group phase of a single edition of the global tournament.

articles.headline: Viswanathan Anand honoured with SJFI Medal
articles.summary: Five-time World chess champion Viswanathan Anand was honoured with the Sports Journalists’ Federation of India’s ‘SJFI Medal’ in Chennai. Anand, also FIDE deputy president, expressed pride in being a catalyst for chess growth in India, calling the award 'a recognition by his peers for real achievements'.

articles.headline: India pips Sri Lanka, seals women’s u-19 T20 series
articles.summary: India clinched the three-match women’s under-19 T20 series with an 11-run win over Sri Lanka in the second game in Chennai. Medium-pacer Janhavi Virkar (3/14) and left-arm spinner Maitri Maniar (3/26) pulled things back for India after Sri Lanka's strong second-wicket partnership.

articles.headline: Standoff between Manika Batra and TTFI intensifies
articles.summary: The standoff between Manika Batra and the Table Tennis Federation of India (TTFI) intensified over her exclusion from the Asian Games squad. Batra hinted at legal recourse, while TTFI explained her omission was based on a cumulative assessment of performance and suitability, not a single factor.

articles.headline: Vanshika and Shiva win 10m air pistol mixed team silver
articles.summary: Vanshika Chaudhary and Shiva Narwal clinched the 10m air pistol mixed team silver medal at the ISSF Junior World Championship in Suhl, Germany. They shot 469.7, finishing behind Poland. India continues to top the medal standings with 16 medals.

articles.headline: Treesa-Gayatri duo advances at US Open badminton
articles.summary: The women’s doubles pair of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopi Chand advanced to the second round of the US Open Super 300 badminton tournament. They overcame Spain’s Paula Lopez and Lucia Rodriguez in a three-game battle, with Treesa making her return after an ankle injury layoff.

articles.headline: Shileima to lead Indian junior women’s hockey team
articles.summary: Midfielder Khaidem Shileima Chanu will lead a 24-member Indian junior women’s hockey team for an exposure tour of the United Kingdom from July 5 to 14. Under new coach Tim White, the team will play seven matches in Scotland and England.

articles.headline: Indian men’s foil team secures Asian Games spot
articles.summary: Japan won gold in the men’s foil team event at the 26th Asian senior fencing championships. The Indian team secured eighth position, directly qualifying for the upcoming Asian Games. South Korea captured gold in women’s epee.

articles.headline: Learn how to win: women’s hockey coach Marijne
articles.summary: Indian women’s hockey coach Sjoerd Marijne emphasized the importance of learning to win, despite the team's FIH Nations Cup victory. He noted the team's improved level but highlighted the need to convert chances into goals. Marijne also stressed increased connection and openness among players.

articles.headline: Anushka, Dev Meena, Jyothi book Asiad tickets
articles.summary: Eighteen-year-old hammer thrower Anushka Yadav broke a nine-year-old National record with 67.02m, while pole vaulter Dev Meena bettered the National record with 5.46m. Hurdler Jyothi Yarraji made a stunning comeback from injury, clocking 12.99s, with all three qualifying for the Asian Games.

articles.headline: Gulveer still in with a chance for Asian Games squad
articles.summary: Gulveer Singh, despite clocking 13:52.92 in the men’s 5,000m, outside the Asiad qualifying mark, still has a chance for the Asian Games squad. The 10,000m bronze medallist's consistency and upcoming decision by the AFI selection committee keep his hopes alive for both 5,000m and 10,000m races.

articles.headline: Argentina’s Messi-ah keeps the fire burning
articles.summary: Lionel Messi, turning 39, scored his 17th and 18th World Cup goals, becoming the singular holder of the record for most World Cup strikes. His brace against Austria, four years after Qatar, raises hopes for an encore. Messi's longevity, razor-sharp brain, and MLS move aid his extraordinary career.

articles.headline: Kingmakers: Insects that make India’s favourite mangoes
articles.summary: A study found that wild insects dramatically increased mango yield by 350%, highlighting their importance for pollination. Native bees, vital for transferring pollen, are threatened by neonicotinoid insecticides used to protect crops from pests. Researchers suggest increasing natural areas near orchards to attract more wild insects.

articles.headline: Gold miners keep working despite Ebola crisis in DRC
articles.summary: In Mongbwalu, DRC, the epicentre of an Ebola outbreak, artisanal gold miners continue their work despite fear of the virus, driven by economic necessity. The outbreak, caused by the Bundibugyo strain, has infected 209 and killed 89 in Mongbwalu. Public mistrust leads many to consult traditional healers instead of hospitals.

articles.headline: Trump dismissed own govt data to slap tariffs on India
articles.summary: Internal reports reveal US President Donald Trump ignored data from his trade representative showing India's tariffs were lower than he claimed. Trump pushed for 150% duties on Indian luxury goods, dismissing official figures as 'bullshit'. The revelations come from a new book detailing Trump's 'America First' trade policy and his confrontational stance against Indian trade practices.

articles.headline: SC: Private medical colleges can charge higher fees than government
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that private medical colleges cannot be prevented from charging higher fees than government institutions, provided they are not capitation fees. The bench stated that fee structure is a matter of management rights under the Constitution. This came during a plea by an EWS student in Puducherry seeking admission at a government-set fee in a private college.

articles.headline: Sensex gains 790 points as crude oil drops below $75
articles.summary: Indian stock markets surged as the Sensex rose 790 points, driven by falling global crude oil prices and strong buying in financial and IT sectors. Brent crude fell below $75 per barrel, easing inflation concerns for India, a major importer. Foreign institutional investors turned net buyers, providing further momentum to the benchmark indices amid positive global cues and domestic stability.

articles.headline: Bengaluru triple murder accused arrested in Puducherry after 36 hours
articles.summary: Police arrested M Shwetha in Puducherry for the brutal murder of her parents and sister in Bengaluru. Shwetha, who allegedly committed the crime with her partner over a dispute regarding her lifestyle, had been on the run for 36 hours. Her partner remains at large. Investigators used mobile tracking and CCTV footage to locate her, ending a massive manhunt across neighboring states.

articles.headline: ED raids senior excise officer in Karnataka wealth case
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate conducted raids at multiple locations linked to a senior excise official in Bengaluru as part of a money-laundering probe. The officer is accused of accumulating assets disproportionate to his known sources of income. Documents related to property investments and bank lockers were seized. The raids are part of a wider crackdown on corruption in state departments.

articles.headline: BJP seeks audit of Gruhalakshmi scheme over ₹108 crore scam
articles.summary: The BJP has demanded a CAG audit of the Karnataka government's Gruhalakshmi scheme, alleging financial irregularities worth ₹108 crore. A preliminary audit reportedly found thousands of ineligible beneficiaries and ghost accounts receiving monthly payments. The opposition claims the welfare funds are being siphoned off through systemic loopholes, while the state govt defends the program's integrity.

articles.headline: NEET paper leak shouldn't have happened, says Education Minister
articles.summary: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan admitted that the NEET-UG paper leak was a failure and 'should not have happened'. He assured students that a high-level committee is being formed to overhaul the National Testing Agency (NTA). Pradhan rejected opposition calls for his resignation, accusing Rahul Gandhi of trying to create anarchy by politicizing the competitive exam crisis.

articles.headline: HC permits 15-year-old rape survivor to terminate 30-week pregnancy
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court allowed a 15-year-old rape survivor to terminate her 30-week pregnancy, prioritizing her mental and physical health over legal limits. Doctors had advised that continuing the pregnancy would cause severe psychological trauma to the minor. The court directed the state to provide necessary medical care and ensure the survivor's privacy throughout the procedure.

articles.headline: Bengaluru hosts 7 of India’s youngest high-value startups
articles.summary: Bengaluru has emerged as the leading hub for India's youngest high-value companies, housing 7 of the top 10 firms on the 2025 Hurun India list. Companies like Zepto, Cred, and Sarvam AI are driving the city's dominance in technology and fintech. The report highlights Bengaluru's robust ecosystem for entrepreneurship, attracting significant venture capital and top-tier engineering talent.

articles.headline: Ukraine strikes major Russian gas plant in night raid
articles.summary: Ukrainian forces conducted a successful drone strike on a significant natural gas processing plant deep inside Russian territory. The attack also reportedly disabled two key satellite communications centers used by the Russian military. Kyiv stated the operation aims to disrupt the energy supplies that fuel the Russian war machine and hinder its tactical coordination on the front lines.

articles.headline: NCERT to introduce ancient civilizations in Class 9 textbooks
articles.summary: NCERT has revised its Class 9 history textbooks to include more comprehensive sections on ancient world civilizations, including Mesopotamia, Egypt, and China. The new curriculum aims to provide students with a broader perspective on global history. The change replaces sections on modern revolutions like the French and Russian revolutions, which have been moved to higher grades.

articles.headline: Karnataka beer sales surge 59% after new alcohol tax
articles.summary: Beer sales in Karnataka jumped by 59% in early June following a shift in the state's excise policy that lowered taxes on mild alcoholic beverages. The government's move to a volume-based tax system has made beer more affordable compared to hard liquor. Total excise revenue, however, remained stable as higher volumes of beer compensated for a slight dip in Indian Made Liquor (IML) sales.

articles.headline: Karnataka reserves 12.5% liquor licenses for SC/ST traders
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has announced a 12.5% reservation for SC/ST individuals in the allotment of new liquor retail licenses. The policy aims to promote social inclusion and entrepreneurship among marginalized communities. While social activists welcomed the move, some existing traders have raised concerns about the impact on market competition and the potential for 'benami' operations.

articles.headline: IPS officer MN Anucheth given dual role in police reshuffle
articles.summary: In a minor administrative reshuffle, senior IPS officer MN Anucheth has been given dual charge as DIG (Internal Security Division) and DIG (Prisons). The move is part of the state's effort to streamline security operations and improve prison management. Several other mid-level officers were also transferred to new roles across various districts to fill long-standing vacancies.

articles.headline: Namma Metro disruption highlights communication and last-mile gaps
articles.summary: A technical glitch on Bengaluru's Namma Metro Purple Line left thousands of commuters stranded during peak hours, exposing major flaws in the BMRCL's emergency response. The lack of timely announcements and inadequate bus shuttle services forced many to walk on tracks or pay exorbitant fares to private cabs. BMRCL has promised a thorough probe into the power failure and its aftermath.

articles.headline: R Ashoka: 19,000 beneficiaries linked to same bank account
articles.summary: BJP leader R Ashoka alleged a massive scam in state welfare schemes, claiming that 19,000 different beneficiaries were linked to just one bank account. This anomaly, flagged in a recent audit, suggests widespread ghost registrations and siphoning of funds. Ashoka demanded a judicial probe, while the government stated it is already investigating the data mismatch and will take strict action.

articles.headline: No hawkers on major roads; vehicles on footpaths to be towed
articles.summary: Bengaluru civic authorities announced a strict ban on street vending along 20 major arterial roads to ease traffic congestion. Additionally, the traffic police will start towing any vehicles parked on footpaths from July 1. The drive is part of the 'Safe Footpath' initiative, aiming to reclaim pedestrian space and reduce road accidents caused by obstructed walkways.

articles.headline: Woman alleges abuse by cab driver over kombucha bottle
articles.summary: A Bengaluru woman filed a police complaint against an app-based cab driver for verbal abuse and physical intimidation. The driver allegedly mistook her bottle of kombucha for beer and tried to force her out of the vehicle in a deserted area. The cab aggregator has suspended the driver pending an investigation. Police are reviewing dashcam footage to verify the sequence of events.

articles.headline: HC denies custody of 9 dogs to 'abusive' owner
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court refused to grant custody of nine dogs to their owner after PETA provided video evidence of the animals being beaten and kept in unsanitary conditions. The court ruled that the owner's 'right to property' over the pets does not override the animals' right to live without cruelty. The dogs will remain in the care of a government-recognized shelter for rehabilitation.

articles.headline: PG owner's final words before dying: I'll return after stitches
articles.summary: A paying guest owner in Bengaluru, who was brutally attacked by two residents over a trivial row, told his son he would be back soon while being taken to the hospital. He succumbed to his injuries shortly after. The police have arrested the two suspects, who were reportedly under the influence of alcohol. The incident has sparked a debate over security and safety in PG accommodations.

articles.headline: Courier agent's organ donation gives five people a second chance
articles.summary: The family of a 40-year-old courier agent, who was declared brain dead following a road accident in Bengaluru, decided to donate his organs. His kidneys, liver, and heart valves were transplanted into five patients across different hospitals. The move was hailed as a noble gesture, especially as the donor was the sole breadwinner for his family of four.

articles.headline: HC: Second wife and child entitled to motor accident relief
articles.summary: In a significant ruling, the Karnataka High Court held that a second wife and her child are entitled to claim compensation under the Motor Vehicles Act, regardless of the legality of the second marriage. The court stated that 'dependents' should be interpreted broadly to ensure social justice. This comes in a case where an insurance company had challenged a claim by a deceased man's second family.

articles.headline: Ex-chief of govt agency ordered to repay ₹44L for fraud
articles.summary: A Bengaluru court directed the former head of a state handicrafts corporation to repay ₹44 lakh after it was found he held the post using a fake ID. The official had also siphoned off funds through fraudulent travel bills and unauthorized expenditures. The court ordered the immediate recovery of the amount with interest, failing which his personal properties will be attached.

articles.headline: BJP hunts for cross-voters; analysts call it a futile exercise
articles.summary: The Karnataka BJP is attempting to identify members who cross-voted in the recent legislative council elections. However, political analysts suggest that given the secret ballot system, proving such allegations will be nearly impossible. The internal probe is seen as a move to instill discipline and deter future rebellions within the party's state unit following recent electoral setbacks.

articles.headline: Nikhil Kumaraswamy demands apology from Minister Patil over land row
articles.summary: JD(S) leader Nikhil Kumaraswamy has sought a public apology from Minister MB Patil for alleged defamatory remarks regarding his family's land holdings. Patil had accused the Deve Gowda family of illegal encroachments in Ramanagara. Nikhil denied the charges, claiming all properties were legally acquired and that the minister's statements were politically motivated to malign his family.

articles.headline: Welfare clean-up could improve finances but poses political risks
articles.summary: The Karnataka government's plan to weed out ineligible beneficiaries from its 'guarantee' schemes could save crores but risks a public backlash. The audit has already identified thousands of duplicate or wealthy recipients. While the fiscal health of the state would improve, political experts warn that any mass deletions could alienate voters ahead of the crucial local body elections.

articles.headline: Simpler rules soon for registration of gramathana properties
articles.summary: The state government is set to simplify the process for regularizing and registering 'gramathana' (village residential) properties. New rules will allow owners to get valid title deeds without lengthy legal hurdles, provided they meet basic criteria. This move is expected to benefit lakhs of rural residents and increase the revenue collected from property taxes in gram panchayats.

articles.headline: Zoo Authority frames new SOP for staff safety and hygiene
articles.summary: Following recent incidents of animal attacks, the Zoo Authority of Karnataka has introduced a new Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for all zoo personnel. The rules mandate strict safety protocols for entering cages and emphasize high standards of hygiene to prevent zoonotic diseases. Staff training programs will also be overhauled to ensure better crisis management during emergencies.

articles.headline: UP youth held in Davangere over Ram Mandir attack plot
articles.summary: Security agencies arrested a youth from Uttar Pradesh in Davangere for allegedly plotting an attack on the Ram Mandir in Ayodhya. The suspect was reportedly in touch with extremist groups across the border via encrypted messaging apps. Police recovered maps and radical literature from his possession. He has been remanded to custody for further interrogation to uncover his local handlers.

articles.headline: Man killed by brother, aides who staged it as accident
articles.summary: A man was murdered by his own brother and three accomplices over a property dispute in rural Bengaluru. The accused initially tried to pass off the death as a hit-and-run road accident. However, the post-mortem report and forensic evidence revealed strangulation marks. All four suspects have been arrested and have reportedly confessed to the crime during police questioning.

articles.headline: Dog lover rewards villager ₹50,000 for finding missing pet
articles.summary: In a heartwarming story from Polya, a dog owner rewarded a local villager with ₹50,000 for finding and returning his pet dog that had been missing for a week. The owner had put up posters and announced the reward across several villages. The villager found the dog in a neighboring district and contacted the owner, who immediately honored the promised reward as a token of gratitude.

articles.headline: C'garh village ousts Christian families; officials broker compromise
articles.summary: Authorities in Chhattisgarh intervened after a village council forced several Christian families to leave over religious differences. A compromise was reached allowing the families to return on the condition they do not hold public prayers and follow local customs. The standoff highlights ongoing communal tensions in tribal areas regarding religious conversions and traditional practices.

articles.headline: IIT-Madras introduces 'Protein Mess' to boost student fitness
articles.summary: IIT-Madras has launched a new dining initiative called 'Protein Mess' to provide students with high-protein, nutritionally balanced meals. The facility aims to support the physical fitness and mental well-being of students, especially those involved in sports. The menu, designed by nutritionists, includes a variety of plant and animal protein sources to cater to different dietary needs.

articles.headline: Airlines turn to AI to save fuel; IndiGo starts trials
articles.summary: Major Indian airlines like IndiGo are starting trials of AI-powered software to optimize fuel consumption during flights. The technology analyzes real-time weather, air traffic, and aircraft performance to recommend the most efficient routes and altitudes. This move is expected to significantly reduce carbon emissions and operational costs for carriers in the competitive aviation market.

articles.headline: Pune realtor pushed off cliff by lover and her accomplice
articles.summary: Police in Pune arrested a woman and her partner for the murder of a real estate agent who was allegedly pushed off a cliff during a trek. The woman had been in a relationship with the victim but reportedly plotted the murder with her new lover to clear personal debts. The crime was uncovered after inconsistencies were found in the woman's initial statement about the victim's disappearance.

articles.headline: Man gets 20 years' rigorous imprisonment for raping minor
articles.summary: A special POCSO court in Gujarat sentenced a 21-year-old man to 20 years of rigorous imprisonment for the rape of a minor girl. The court noted the gravity of the offense and the psychological impact on the victim. In addition to the jail term, the convict was ordered to pay a fine, which will be given to the victim as compensation for her medical and rehabilitation needs.

articles.headline: Tejpal case: Why was two-word text sent the next day?
articles.summary: During the appeal against Tarun Tejpal's acquittal, the prosecution questioned the timing of a two-word text message sent by the journalist to the survivor the day after the alleged assault. The court is examining whether the message constituted an admission of guilt or was taken out of context. The high-profile case continues to be heard in the Goa bench of the High Court.

articles.headline: No proof of accident, but car owner wins ₹1.3L claim
articles.summary: A consumer forum ordered an insurance company to pay ₹1.3 lakh to a car owner despite a lack of formal accident proof. The forum ruled that the company's rejection of the claim was based on 'technicalities' and that the damage to the vehicle was consistent with the owner's account. The ruling emphasizes the consumer's right to fair treatment by insurance providers in genuine cases.

articles.headline: NCERT delays Nazism and French Revolution for Class 9 students
articles.summary: NCERT has decided to postpone the teaching of Nazism and the French Revolution in the Class 9 history curriculum by one year. The topics will now be covered in Class 10 to allow for a more balanced workload. The change is part of a wider effort to streamline the social science syllabus and incorporate more chapters on ancient Indian history and the development of local cultures.

articles.headline: Bengal eyes stringent law to fight organized crime syndicates
articles.summary: The West Bengal government is planning to introduce a new law similar to MCOCA to tackle organized crime and syndicates in the state. The proposed legislation will give police more powers to detain suspects and attach properties of those involved in extortion and illegal land deals. The move follows a series of high-profile crimes linked to powerful local 'syndicates' in urban areas.

articles.headline: India calls for Brics shared space economy for mutual growth
articles.summary: During a Brics summit, India proposed the creation of a 'shared space economy' among member nations. The initiative would involve joint satellite projects, data sharing for disaster management, and collaboration on deep-space exploration. The proposal aims to leverage the combined technological expertise of the Brics countries to reduce costs and accelerate progress in the space sector.

articles.headline: BMC staffer falls into flooded open drain during inspection
articles.summary: A Mumbai civic staffer fell into an open, flooded drain during an inspection by the city's mayor. The incident, caught on camera, highlighted the poor state of Mumbai's drainage system just before the peak monsoon. The staffer was rescued by colleagues with minor injuries. The mayor has ordered an immediate audit of all open drains and manholes to prevent similar accidents.

articles.headline: Assam floods: Arunachal rains leave 1 dead as alert sounds
articles.summary: Heavy rainfall in Arunachal Pradesh has triggered flash floods in Assam, leading to the death of one person and displacing thousands. Several rivers are flowing above the danger mark, inundating villages and damaging standing crops. The state government has set up relief camps and deployed SDRF teams for rescue operations. An orange alert has been issued for the next 48 hours.

articles.headline: Sikka’s AI startup Hang Ten raises $32 million in seed funding
articles.summary: Hang Ten, an AI startup founded by former Infosys CEO Vishal Sikka, has raised $32 million in its first seed funding round. The company focuses on developing AI solutions for large enterprises to improve operational efficiency. The funding will be used to expand its engineering team and scale its global operations, reflecting strong investor interest in Sikka's latest technological venture.

articles.headline: Mahindra: IT services will matter more in the AI era
articles.summary: Tech Mahindra's leadership emphasized that the demand for IT services will increase as companies seek to integrate AI into their business models. Contrary to fears of job losses, the firm believes AI will create new opportunities in data management and software development. Mahindra stated that Indian IT firms are well-positioned to lead this global transition through skilled talent.

articles.headline: RBI seeks more time to redress e-fraud complaints
articles.summary: The RBI has asked for an extension of the deadline for banks to resolve e-fraud complaints from customers. Currently, banks are required to settle most disputes within 30 days, but the central bank argues that complex cyber-fraud cases often require more time for thorough investigation. The move aims to ensure that customers get fair outcomes while reducing the burden on bank staff.

articles.headline: UP secures ₹50,000 crore investment deals in Bengaluru summit
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government signed MoUs worth ₹50,000 crore with several tech and manufacturing firms during an investment summit in Bengaluru. The deals focus on data centers, electronics manufacturing, and green energy. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath promised a business-friendly environment and single-window clearances to ensure the rapid implementation of these projects in the state.

articles.headline: Munoz emerges as Colombia's hero in narrow win
articles.summary: Daniel Munoz scored the only goal as Colombia defeated DR Congo in a tightly contested World Cup match. The victory puts Colombia in a strong position to advance to the next round. Despite missing several key players through injury, the Colombian defense held firm against a late Congolese surge, securing a vital three points in their quest for global glory.

articles.headline: England stunned as Africans secure historic win under Tuchel
articles.summary: England suffered a shock defeat as Ghana secured a historic 1-0 win in their World Cup group clash. This was Thomas Tuchel's first loss as England manager, sparking immediate criticism of his tactical choices. Ghana's disciplined defense and clinical counter-attack exposed weaknesses in the English side, leaving the Three Lions needing a win in their next game to avoid an early exit.

articles.headline: Why all final World Cup group games are played simultaneously
articles.summary: FIFA's long-standing rule of playing final group matches at the same time is designed to prevent 'match-fixing' where teams could play for a mutually beneficial result. This practice ensures fair play and maintains the integrity of the tournament. The rule was established following a controversial match in 1982 and remains a cornerstone of the World Cup's competitive format.

articles.headline: Germany midwife looks to secure three wins out of three
articles.summary: Germany's national team is aiming for a perfect group stage record as they prepare for their final match against Ivory Coast. Manager Thomas Tuchel's side has already qualified for the knockouts but wants to maintain momentum. The German team has shown impressive form so far, with a strong defense and a prolific strike force making them one of the tournament's top contenders.

articles.headline: Curaçao eye major upset against Ivory Coast in World Cup
articles.summary: The underdog team from Curaçao is hoping to cause a major upset as they face a powerful Ivory Coast side in a must-win World Cup match. Despite the massive difference in rankings, Curaçao's manager believes his team can exploit defensive weaknesses in the African side. A win would be historic for the small Caribbean island nation and could potentially see them advance to the next round.

articles.headline: Sweden vows to stay compact against organized Japan side
articles.summary: Sweden's national football team is preparing a defensive strategy to counter Japan's technical and fast-paced playing style in their upcoming World Cup clash. The Swedish coach emphasized the need for a compact midfield and disciplined positioning to stop Japan's creative playmakers. Both teams are fighting for the top spot in their group, making this a high-stakes encounter.

articles.headline: Jyothi Yarraji shines as records fall at National Athletics
articles.summary: Star hurdler Jyothi Yarraji broke her own national record in the 100m hurdles at the National Inter-State Athletics Championships. Other athletes like Anushka and Meena also set new national marks in their respective events, highlighting a successful day for Indian athletics. The performance is a major boost for the contingent ahead of upcoming international competitions including the Asian Games.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Blasters survive scare in local football league
articles.summary: The Bengaluru Blasters managed to secure a narrow 2-1 victory over their rivals in a local football league match. Despite taking an early lead, the Blasters faced a spirited comeback from the opposition and were forced to defend deeply in the final minutes. The win keeps them at the top of the league table, but the coach warned that the team needs to improve its finishing in front of goal.

articles.headline: MNR Rao Park ease to comfortable victory in basketball
articles.summary: MNR Rao Park's basketball team secured a comfortable 61-29 win in the Super League of the State Youth (U-16) Basketball Championship. Their dominant performance was led by key players who excelled in both offense and defense. This victory puts them in a strong position for the semi-finals, as they continue their unbeaten run in the tournament against some of the city's top youth teams.

articles.headline: Pew survey shows Trump's global approval ratings tanking
articles.summary: A new survey by the Pew Research Center reveals that global approval ratings for US President Donald Trump have reached a record low. The survey, conducted across 36 nations, shows a significant decline in confidence in US leadership among key allies in Europe and Asia. Many respondents cited Trump's trade policies and his approach to international agreements as primary reasons for their concern.

articles.headline: US SC ruling raises travel risks for green card holders
articles.summary: A recent US Supreme Court ruling has created new legal hurdles for green card holders traveling abroad. The court held that non-citizens, including permanent residents, could face re-entry challenges if they have certain past legal issues, even if they were previously cleared. Legal experts advise green card holders to seek counsel before international travel to avoid being barred from returning.

articles.headline: Independent UN panel says Israel deliberately targeted Palestinian children
articles.summary: An independent UN commission has accused the Israeli military of deliberately targeting Palestinian children during recent conflicts in Gaza and the West Bank. The panel's report cites evidence of systemic human rights violations and calls for an international investigation into potential war crimes. Israel has rejected the findings, claiming its military operations target only extremist groups.

articles.headline: Swiss cinemas offer seniors refuge from record heatwave
articles.summary: Cinemas across Switzerland are offering free or discounted tickets to senior citizens to provide them with a cool refuge as the country experiences a record-breaking heatwave. Temperatures have soared above 35°C in several regions, posing a health risk to the elderly. The initiative has been widely praised by health officials as an effective way to prevent heat-related illnesses and deaths.

articles.headline: An Ajmer legend whistles past: The Railway Workshop turns 150
articles.summary: The Ajmer Railway Workshop, which produced India's first indigenous locomotive, is celebrating its 150th anniversary. The facility played a crucial role in the development of the Indian Railways and the growth of Ajmer as a major industrial hub. Today, the workshop continues to innovate, focusing on the maintenance of modern high-speed trains while preserving its rich engineering heritage.

This dedication has led to multiple national titles and a promising future in international competitions.

articles.headline: Modi urges states to roll out 'e-Zero FIRs' for cyber fraud

This move is intended to speed up the registration process and allow for quicker freezing of fraudulent accounts by law enforcement agencies. The PM emphasized that a unified digital reporting system is essential to tackle rising cybercrime.

articles.headline: Warehouse collapse kills five workers in Kolkata, 18 feared trapped
articles.summary: Five workers died and at least 18 are feared trapped after an under-construction warehouse in Kolkata's Taratala area collapsed. The Indian Army and NDRF joined rescue efforts. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee ordered an audit of construction sites under KMC and announced compensation. Three people have been detained for interrogation in connection with the incident.

articles.headline: India and US discuss ways to ink interim trade deal
articles.summary: Trade officials from India and the US met to discuss the framework for an interim trade deal to resolve long-standing disputes. The discussions focused on market access for agricultural products and easing tariffs on high-tech exports. While a final agreement is months away, both sides expressed optimism about reaching a limited deal that would boost bilateral trade and strengthen economic ties.

articles.headline: Explainer: What documents constitute valid proof of citizenship in India?
articles.summary: Legal experts clarify that documents like Voter IDs, Aadhar cards, and passports are not definitive proof of Indian citizenship. While these documents establish identity or nationality for travel, citizenship is primarily governed by the Citizenship Act of 1955. Verification often requires birth records or ancestry documents, leading to calls for a more streamlined national citizenship document.

articles.headline: Pilots and ATCO grounded after AI flight enters Pakistan airspace
articles.summary: Air India pilots and an Air Traffic Controller were grounded after a flight from Delhi to Amritsar accidentally entered Pakistan's airspace. The deviation was reportedly due to a miscommunication between the cockpit and ground control during bad weather. An internal inquiry has been launched to determine the exact cause of the breach and prevent any future diplomatic or security issues.

articles.headline: Cristiano Ronaldo's double leads Portugal to victory
articles.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice to help Portugal secure a vital victory in their World Cup group stage match. The veteran striker's performance silenced critics and propelled his team toward the knockout rounds. The win makes Portugal one of the favorites for the title, with Ronaldo continuing to break international goal-scoring records and lead his young teammates from the front.

articles.headline: France detects its first confirmed Ebola case in Paris
articles.summary: French health authorities have confirmed the country's first case of Ebola in a patient who recently arrived from Bunia, DR Congo. The World Health Organization is monitoring the situation, noting that the patient was immediately isolated and is receiving treatment in a specialized hospital. Officials stated that while the patient’s condition is serious, the risk of transmission remains low.

articles.headline: Amazon chief Jassy focuses on Indian quick-commerce market
articles.summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy highlighted India as a critical market for the company's expansion into quick-commerce. Jassy stated that Amazon is looking to compete with local players like Zepto and Blinkit by offering 15-minute deliveries in major Indian cities. The company plans to invest significantly in local warehouse networks and logistics to capture a share of the rapidly growing segment.

articles.headline: India faces Bangladesh in crucial T20 World Cup semifinal race
articles.summary: The Indian cricket team is set to face Bangladesh in a high-stakes T20 World Cup match. Following a recent loss to South Africa, India must win this encounter to stay in contention for a semifinal spot. Players like Shafali Verma and Shreyanka Patil are expected to play key roles. The match is viewed as a significant test of India’s momentum in the latter stages of the tournament.

articles.headline: India aims for 10GW nuclear power capacity by 2035
articles.summary: The government has set an ambitious target to reach 10GW of nuclear power capacity by 2035 as part of its clean energy transition. This will involve the construction of several new indigenous reactors and collaboration with international partners for small modular reactors. The move is aimed at reducing the country's carbon footprint and providing a stable base-load for the national grid.

articles.headline: Five family members drown in Cauvery river during Mandya trip
articles.summary: Five members of a family, including children, drowned in the Cauvery river at a pilgrimage site in Mandya district, Karnataka. The victims had entered the water to bathe after offering prayers at a local temple when they were swept away by strong currents. Local residents and emergency services recovered all five bodies. Police are investigating the incident and warning of rising water levels.

articles.headline: Naidu launches India's largest private gold mining project in Kurnool
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N. Chandrababu Naidu launched the Jonnagiri gold mining project in Kurnool, the largest private sector gold mine in India. The project, developed by Geomysore Services India, involves an investment of over Rs 400 crore. The mine is expected to produce roughly 750 kg of gold annually, contributing to local employment and reducing India's dependence on imported gold.

articles.headline: ED cites FEMA violations in probe of Rajesh Exports
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate has identified significant violations of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) by the jewelry firm Rajesh Exports. Following searches at multiple locations, investigators found evidence of unauthorized financial transactions and discrepancies in accounting for foreign investments. The firm is also accused of misusing banking channels for illegal transfers abroad.

articles.headline: Koppal: New reservoir gates to be inaugurated today by CM
articles.summary: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah will today inaugurate the newly installed crest gates at the Tungabhadra dam in Koppal. The project, completed at a cost of ₹51 crore, replaces old gates that were damaged during last year's monsoon. The new infrastructure is crucial for water management in the region, benefiting farmers in Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Telangana during the upcoming rainy season.

articles.headline: Punjab CM Mann attacks rivals over use of fake defaming video
articles.summary: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann accused opposition parties of using AI-generated "fake" videos to tarnish his reputation and defame his government. Mann claimed the videos were circulated on social media to spread misinformation and influence public opinion. He warned of legal action against those responsible for creating and sharing manipulated content for political gain.

articles.headline: Sena (UBT) MPs meet Speaker to seek copy of rebel demands
articles.summary: Members of Parliament from the Shiv Sena (UBT) faction met with the Lok Sabha Speaker to request copies of the formal demands submitted by six rebel party members. The move is part of the ongoing legal and political dispute regarding the party's leadership and the disqualification of defecting members. The Speaker has stated that the requests are under review by the constitutional authorities.

articles.headline: RBI tightens NBFC norms offering no respite for Tata Sons
articles.summary: The RBI's updated scale-based regulations for Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) have reinforced the requirement for Tata Sons to transition into a listed entity. By lowering the asset-size threshold for the upper-layer category to ₹1 lakh crore, the central bank has closed potential loopholes, forcing large holding companies to comply with stricter public listing and disclosure standards.

articles.headline: Goyal to visit U.K. to review trade deal preparedness
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal will visit the U.K. to meet counterpart Peter Kyle and review preparedness for the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) and Double Contribution Convention (DCC) implementation on July 15. Discussions will cover regulatory roadmaps, customs coordination, and market access.

articles.headline: RBI Governor: Premature to talk of interest rate hike
articles.summary: RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das stated it is premature to consider a hike in interest rates despite ongoing inflationary pressures. Speaking at a business event, he noted that while core inflation is under control, food prices remain volatile due to geopolitical factors. The central bank will maintain its 'wait and watch' approach to ensure sustainable economic growth without fueling inflation.

articles.headline: Info Edge shifts focus to AI, deeptech
articles.summary: Sanjeev Bikhchandani’s Info Edge allocated nearly half its startup investments over 12 months to deeptech and AI, pivoting from consumer tech. Since 2020, it invested ₹455 crore in 30 deeptech companies and ₹614 crore in 28 AI startups, achieving gross IRRs of 15% and 31% respectively, seeing these as strategic and transformative sectors for India.

articles.headline: Trump defends Iran funds release as Senate rebukes war move
articles.summary: President Trump defended the release of Iranian funds, claiming they'd buy US food/medical supplies, despite Iran's dispute. This follows a Senate vote to end the US war with Iran, a symbolic rebuke of Trump's strategy. Critics fear Iran will use funds for military rebuilding, while Trump dismisses concerns as "wrong."

articles.headline: Noel Tata to retire as Chairman of retail giant Trent
articles.summary: Noel Tata has announced his retirement as Chairman of Trent, marking the end of a historic tenure that saw the company's transformation into a leading Indian fashion retailer. Under his leadership, Trent expanded brands like Westside and Zudio across the country. He will transition to a non-executive role on the board as part of a planned leadership succession within the Tata Group.

articles.headline: Trade Negotiations Progress Between Key Nations
articles.summary: Important trade negotiations between several key nations are showing progress, with expectations of new agreements that could boost international commerce.

articles.headline: Sports Federation Unveils New Talent Development Program
articles.summary: The national sports federation has launched a new talent development program, aimed at nurturing young athletes and improving performance in international competitions.

articles.headline: U.P. fire: LDA recommends action against 25 officials for lapses
articles.summary: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) has recommended strict action against 25 current and former officials, including six Provincial Civil Service officers, in connection with a building fire that killed 15 people. The officials held charge as zonal officers and engineers, deemed responsible for safety norm violations.

articles.headline: Afghan women turn to entrepreneurship amid restrictions
articles.summary: Amid Taliban's draconian restrictions, Afghan women are increasingly turning to small-scale entrepreneurship as universities and formal workplaces become inaccessible. Data shows a significant increase in women-owned businesses, though many operate informally and face challenges like limited access to finance, often relying on family networks.

articles.headline: IAEA chief confirms Iran nuclear site inspections to resume soon
articles.summary: IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi announced that international inspectors will soon resume monitoring of Iran's nuclear facilities. This follows a high-level agreement on a 14-point memorandum of understanding to resolve long-standing safeguards issues. While no final deal is inked, the move signals a temporary easing of tensions between the UN watchdog and Tehran over its enrichment program.

articles.headline: BJP accuses Kharges of land grab; rejects charges against Mohan Yadav
articles.summary: The BJP has leveled allegations of land grabbing against the family of Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge in Karnataka. Simultaneously, the party rejected similar charges against its own leader, Mohan Yadav, in Madhya Pradesh. The exchange has sparked a political war of words, with both sides accusing each other of using administrative power to facilitate illegal property deals for family members.

articles.headline: Iran invites PM Modi to Khamenei's funeral; high-level rep likely
articles.summary: Tehran has formally invited Prime Minister Narendra Modi to attend the funeral of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. Given the geopolitical sensitivity, India is expected to send a high-level representative, possibly the Vice President or External Affairs Minister, to pay respects. The move comes as Iran seeks to maintain strong ties with regional powers during a period of leadership transition.

articles.headline: Man stabbed to death on Mumbai local train over door row
articles.summary: A 30-year-old man was fatally stabbed on a Mumbai suburban train following a heated argument with a fellow passenger over a closing door. The victim died at the scene despite attempts by commuters to help. The assailant was apprehended by railway police at the next station. The incident has raised fresh concerns about passenger safety and the lack of security personnel on local trains.

articles.headline: Ante Budimir's late winner keeps Croatia in World Cup fray
articles.summary: A late goal by Ante Budimir gave Croatia a crucial 1-0 win over Panama, keeping their World Cup hopes alive. After a frustrating match with several missed chances, Budimir's header in the 88th minute secured the three points. Croatia now needs a win in their final group match to advance, while Panama is officially knocked out of the tournament following their second straight loss.

articles.headline: Mamdani's picks sweep New York races as anti-establishment anger grows
articles.summary: Candidates backed by Zohran Mamdani achieved significant victories in recent New York primary elections, reflecting a surge in anti-establishment sentiment. The wins for the progressive wing of the Democratic Party were driven by concerns over housing affordability and healthcare. Analysts suggest this trend could influence the party's national platform ahead of the upcoming general elections.

articles.headline: Forex Exposure Rules Get Looser
articles.summary: Foreign exchange exposure rules for Indian companies have been relaxed. This move is intended to provide greater flexibility for businesses operating in international markets.
